user_query:
Please audit the surgical logs in input/hospital_logs.json. Cross-reference each case's surgeon notes/procedure summary against the billed items to identify revenue leakage (items mentioned but missing from billing or under-billed). Produce the following outputs:

1) A Markdown report saved to output/audit_report.md that includes:
   - A table with the exact header: "Item in Notes | Status in Bill | Estimated Leakage"
   - Line items for each discrepancy with Estimated Leakage shown in INR (₹)
   - A final line stating: "Total Estimated Revenue Recovery: ₹[amount]"
   - A short Methodology section describing how you matched items and estimated leakage
   - An Assumptions & Notes section for any ambiguities (e.g., unclear item matching or missing costs)
   - A WhatsApp Message Draft to the Hospital Administrator requesting approval to update the bill (include the phrase "approval to update the bill")
   - A brief privacy disclaimer confirming patient data has been de-identified

2) A machine-readable JSON at output/discrepancies.json containing:
   {
     "discrepancies": [
       {"item": "...", "status": "missing|under_billed|billed", "estimated_leakage": <number>}
     ],
     "total_estimated_recovery": <number>
   }

Use only the data provided in input/hospital_logs.json. Do not use any external sources. If an item's cost is not explicit, state your estimation approach in the Assumptions & Notes section. Ensure all patient identifiers are omitted or masked in the report and draft.
